title: Payton Tolle’s emergence has been a bright spot amid Red Sox’ uneven 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched six scoreless innings in the Red Sox's 8-1 win over the Baltimore Orioles at Fenway Park.
Tolle achieved his first scoreless start in the majors, maintaining a 2.28 ERA across his eight starts this season.
The Red Sox rookie has delivered five quality starts, limiting opponents to three or fewer runs in 10 of his 11 career starts.

title: Police investigating after $30,000 in landscaping equipment stolen from community college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: $30,000 worth of landscaping equipment was stolen from Massasoit Community College over two days.
Suspects used a stolen silver Ford F-150, license 2SEB87, for three robberies between May 23 and May 25.
Police are seeking information and can be contacted at 508 588-9100 ext. 1037 or 508 894-2600.

title: Hull police prepare for planned ‘large gathering’ at Nantasket Beach
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Hull police to increase staffing Friday for a large gathering at Nantasket Beach, citing recent social media posts.
Hull Police Chief warns against criminal activity, alcohol use, and disorderly behavior at the beach event.
Incidents at other New England beaches have led to violence and police action in recent weeks.

title: American flag burned, then replaced outside Masonic temple in Maine, fire officials says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An American flag was deliberately burned outside a historic Masonic temple in Bath, Maine, on Tuesday morning.
The flag was already replaced with a new one before firefighters arrived, and it's unclear when the fire happened.
Investigators are seeking information on the arson and the unidentified person who replaced the flag.

title: Family of Mass. worker who died after getting trapped in freezer gets nearly $13 million in settlement
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The family of a Massachusetts worker received nearly $13 million in a wrongful death settlement.
The worker died trapped in a freezer, with a failed door handle cited as a key issue in the case.
Expert testimony and extensive investigation led to the settlement without proceeding to trial.

title: Clover plans to reopen some locations after sudden closure, thanks to an anonymous investor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Clover Food Lab to reopen Cambridge and Boston locations on June 9 after securing new investment.
The investment comes after Clover's closure announcement due to financial struggles and an unsuccessful search for a buyer.
Clover plans a reduced footprint focusing on Boston and Cambridge, maintaining its local farm sourcing commitment.

title: Abreu has a two-run homer and 3 RBIs, Tolle pitches 6 scoreless innings and Red Sox beat Orioles 8-1
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit a two-run homer and drove in three runs as the Red Sox defeated the Orioles 8-1.
Payton Tolle pitched six scoreless innings, allowing seven hits and lowering his ERA to 2.28.
Mickey Gasper hit a two-run triple during Boston's five-run fifth inning, with every batting order spot getting a hit.

title: MassDems accuse GOP candidates of intentionally submitting false signatures to make primary ballot
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts Democrats accuse GOP candidates Walsh and Manning-Martin of submitting fraudulent signatures for the September primary ballot.
Democrats demand withdrawal as fraudulent signatures may leave Walsh and Manning-Martin short of the 10,000 needed for ballot eligibility.
GOP candidates, including Walsh, deny wrongdoing, claiming sufficient signatures and political maneuvering by Democrats to undermine them.

title: Isiah Kiner-Falefa, Chad Tracy elaborate on cryptic comments on Red Sox’ Fenway struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Isiah Kiner-Falefa cites distractions at Fenway as a reason for the Red Sox's 9-20 home record.
Interim manager Chad Tracy acknowledges increased media and staff presence at Fenway contributes to the team's struggles.
Red Sox maintain a 16-14 road record, leading to no changes in pregame approach despite home challenges.

title: Teen found dismembered in Chelsea parking lot is identified 26 years later
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tiffany Bradley, a 16-year-old from Pennsylvania, was identified 26 years after her murder in Chelsea, Massachusetts.
Eugene McCollom, already convicted for Bradley's murder, claimed she was a sex worker but she was actually trafficked.
DNA advancements and genealogical profiling helped identify Bradley, providing closure for her family after decades.
